content:
# Recommendations for the next plan iteration (post-iter-234)

## TL;DR
- **d.2 lead lane: the gate is MET — proceed to stage (iv).** `stalkTensorLinearMap` landed axiom-clean (iter-233's review condition). Dispatch the **reverse map** `stalkTensorRev` as the next unit. This is convergence, not churn (2 consecutive iters of concrete forward motion: iter-233 forward map → iter-234 linear packaging).
- **FlatBaseChange engine lane: the tactic `map_smul'` route is DEAD — do NOT retry it.** Switch to the element-free `restrictScalarsComp'App` route (probed, step (a) `rfl`-confirmed).

## Closest-to-completion / prioritize

### 1. d.2 stage (iv) — reverse map `stalkTensorRev` (StalkTensor.lean) — HIGHEST PRIORITY, lead critical path
- **Why now:** stage (iii) is closed; the carrier-duality risk is retired (recipe below). Stage (iv) + (v) are the only thing between the project and `stalkTensorIso` → `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` (unconditional) → `thm:pic_commgroup`.
- **What to build:** `stalkTensorRev : A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x →ₗ[R.stalk x] (A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x` via `TensorProduct.lift` of an `R_x`-bilinear `revBilin (germ_U a) (germ_V b) := germ_{U⊓V}((a|)⊗(b|))` (two nested `colimit.desc` / germ universal property). ~150–250 LOC.
- **Confirmed: NO Mathlib shortcut.** `TensorProduct.directLimit` / `Module.DirectLimit` tensor-commutation is ABSENT — the descent must be hand-built from `TopCat.Presheaf.stalk`. Do not send the prover hunting for a one-line lemma.
- **Reuse the carrier-duality recipe verbatim** (see below) when computing `germ_smul`/`tmul` at section level.
- **Scoping note for the gate:** under mathlib-build (no sorry pins), a half-built reverse map is not committable, so stage (iv) is one prover unit and stage (v) (`stalkTensorIso` bundle, using the already-landed `stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul`) is the next. The counter-moving datapoint (`thm:pic_commgroup`) remains owed until both land — this is the genuine size of the d.2 infra, not a stall. **Guard against fragmentation:** if the next d.2 round adds only more `stalkTensorDescU`-style helpers without landing `stalkTensorRev`, treat the lane as churning and reconsider.

### 2. Blueprint-writer action before the next StalkTensor dispatch (lean-vs-blueprint-checker, 2 major)
- **Add a standalone `lem:stalk_tensor_linearmap` block** in `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` with `\lean{PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorLinearMap}` and the signature `(A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x →ₗ[R.stalk x] A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x`, parallel to the existing `lem:stalk_tensor_desc_forward`. Without it, `sync_leanok` has nothing to mark for the stage-(iii) landing and the stage-(iv) prover has no pinned navigation target. Mention `stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul` in it (stage-v inversion tool).
- The `\lean{stalkTensorIso}` forward-pin on `lem:stalk_tensor_commutation` (targets an unbuilt decl) — I added a `% NOTE: NOT YET BUILT` this iter; the writer should keep that until the iso is assembled. No `\leanok` risk (sync handles it).

## Promising approaches needing more work

### 3. FlatBaseChange Γ-fragment — element-free route (NOT the tactic route)
- **Target:** `moduleSpecΓFunctor_pushforward_tilde_iso`.
- **DEAD — do NOT retry:** the tactic `map_smul'` route (`show φ.hom r • s = r • s`, `change … globalSectionsIso …`, `IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul`). The intermediate `Γ(Spec R,⊤)`/`Γ(Spec R',⊤)` actions are buried in `Module.compHom`/`restrictScalars` and are not synthesizable `SMul` instances on the final carrier — confirmed instance wall.
- **RECOMMENDED:** the element-free `restrictScalarsComp'App` + `restrictScalarsId'App` + `eqToIso`(`ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality`) route — all maps identity-carrier, no element smul touched. Step (a) is `rfl`-confirmed. Remaining: dig the pushforward ring map `ψ : Γ(Spec R,⊤)→Γ(Spec R',⊤)` out of `PresheafOfModules.pushforward = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars` internals (`(Spec.map φ).toRingCatSheafHom` is an `InducedCategory.Hom` — `.val` does not project); and verify `(Spec.map φ)⁻¹ᵁ⊤=⊤` by `rfl` (else insert `eqToHom` transport).
- **Scope caveat — do not over-promise:** even closing this Γ-fragment does NOT close `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`. Still needed: object-level quasi-coherence of the pushforward (Mathlib-absent), pullback dictionary, fibre-product identification `X'=Spec(R'⊗_R A)`, and the `cancelBaseChange` adjoint-mate match. This is a multi-iter engine build — frame the Γ-fragment as a first brick, not a finish line.

## Known blockers — do NOT re-assign as stated
- **`moduleSpecΓFunctor_pushforward_tilde_iso` via tactic `map_smul'`** — instance wall, dead (see above). Only the element-free route is live.
- **`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`** (Vestigial.lean, the d.2 consumer) — remains a sorry; it closes only once `stalkTensorIso` (stages iv+v) exists. Do NOT attempt it before the iso lands.

## Lean-auditor findings (`task_results/lean-auditor-iter234.md`)
- **StalkTensor.lean: clean** — all 4 new decls axiom-clean, genuine, non-vacuous, faithful to blueprint. (Minor: `stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul` is a near-alias of `stalkTensorDesc_germ_tmul` — justified for the `LinearMap`-form callers; no action needed.)
- **FlatBaseChange.lean: 2 "must-fix" sorries flagged** — these are `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` (L213, honest affine-local reduction + sorry) and `flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` (L246, pure sorry, transitively depends on the affine one). **These are the project's known, intentionally-deferred deep engine theorems**, not new regressions — the auditor flags them per its generic "sorry on load-bearing claim" rule. The planner should keep them deferred (the affine one is blocked behind the Γ-fragment + dictionary chain above; the flat one behind a Čech build). Do NOT dispatch a prover to "fill the sorry" directly — they unblock only via the dictionary route in §3.
- **Minor cleanup (FlatBaseChange.lean ~L181):** the `STATUS (iter-234)` section comment embeds environment-specific noise (`MOONSHOT_API_KEY → HTTP 401`) and iteration tags that will stale. Worth a one-line refactor/prover cleanup to strip the env details (keep the mathematical blocker description) — low priority.

## Reusable proof patterns discovered this iter
- **Carrier-duality recipe (CommRingCat `R(U)` scalar vs RingCat-carrier module on `A(U)⊗B(U)`):** never `rw [smul_tmul']` / `map_add` / `map_zero` directly on `ConcreteCategory.hom g` (instance synthesis fails). Instead: `erw [stalkTensorDescU_tmul, germ_smul]` + term-mode `exact (smul_tmul' _ _ _).symm` (tmul); `erw [smul_add]` + `refine (map_add _ _ _).trans ?_` + `congrArg` (add); `simp only [… map_zero, smul_zero]; exact map_zero _` (zero — `r•0` defeq `0`). Applies to stages (iv)/(v).
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false`** required for any iso between `restrictScalars`-of-Spec-modules to even typecheck.
- **Stalk-linearity pattern:** `germ_exist` scalar+vector → common neighbourhood `W=U⊓V` via `germ_res_apply` → chain `← germ_smul, …_germ, …_smul, ← …_germ`. (Mirrors d.1 `stalkLinearMap`.)

## Environment note
- Informal-agent unavailable this iter: `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` → HTTP 401; no `DEEPSEEK`/`OPENROUTER`/`OPENAI`/`GEMINI` key set. Provers should not plan around `archon-informal-agent.py` until a key is restored.
